description: MDDI's S$1B AI research investment announcement — flagship policy doc, structured content, clear date/title.

input.title: Singapore Invests Over S$1 Billion in National AI Research and Development Plan to Strengthen AI Research Capabilities and Our Position as Global AI Hub
input.contentText: 24 January 2026. The Ministry of Digital Development and Information (MDDI) today announced that the Government will invest over S$1 billion in public artificial intelligence (AI) research and development from 2026 to 2030. This investment will strengthen Singapore's position as a global AI hub by deepening research capabilities in three priority areas. First, the Government will fund research on responsible and resource-efficient AI, building on initiatives such as AI Verify. Second, the investment supports a full-pipeline AI talent development effort, from junior college pre-tertiary programmes through to university faculty training. Third, funds will support industry applications to shorten the path from research to commercialisation. This S$1 billion commitment follows the S$500 million invested in 2024 in high-performance computing infrastructure, and signals Singapore's transition from pilot exploration to scaled build-out. On a per-capita basis, Singapore's AI investment will reach US$139, far above the US (US$33) and China (US$7). Minister for Digital Development and Information Mrs Josephine Teo said the funding represents a generational opportunity for Singapore to lead in trustworthy and inclusive AI.
